idea_spark.inspiration: 双RANSAC框架和线向量集的使用为处理局部几何特征提供了新思路。

en.tldr: Proposes a fast point cloud registration algorithm using probabilistic self-updating local correspondence and line vector sets, achieving at least 10% RMSE improvement on public datasets.
en.motivation: Existing point cloud registration methods struggle to balance accuracy and efficiency, necessitating a fast and accurate algorithm.
en.method: Employs a dual RANSAC interaction model with global and local RANSAC, initial local sets built via angle histogram statistics and line vector length preservation, refined by a probabilistic self-updating strategy, and a global early termination condition.
en.result: Outperforms state-of-the-art methods in time efficiency and reduces RMSE by at least 10% on public datasets.
en.help_to_user: Provides an efficient point cloud registration implementation applicable to remote sensing and 3D reconstruction.

idea_spark.idea: Combining dynamic local set updates with a global termination condition balances accuracy and efficiency.
idea_spark.risk: Local set construction may be unstable under high noise or low overlap.
idea_spark.inspiration: The dual RANSAC framework and line vector sets offer new insights for handling local geometric features.

papers.title: Adaptive Self-Organization in Anonymous Dynamic Networks
papers.summary: We introduce the problem of adaptive self-organization in which the nodes of an anonymous, synchronous dynamic network must distributively change the collective distribution of their responses (or "colors") as a function of time-varying environmental signals, even when these signals are only perceived locally and the network topology changes adversarially. Specifically, a signal adversary may change the type of signal and which node(s) witness that signal arbitrarily between rounds. If a signal (or lack thereof) $s$ persists in the system for sufficiently long, the dynamic network must stabilize such that nodes' colors reach and remain in a distribution closely approximating $r(s)$, a goal distribution defined by the problem instance. We first prove that if nodes are deterministic, the only solvable instances of adaptive-self organization are those with homogeneous goal distributions, i.e., those where all nodes must stabilize with the same color. We then present a linear-time, logarithmic-memory, deterministic algorithm for this subclass of instances that works even when the multiplicity and location of signal witnesses change arbitrarily. When nodes know $n$, the number of nodes in the network, a small adaptation of this algorithm achieves a stronger convergence property in which adversarial edge and signal dynamics are entirely unable to disturb stabilized configurations. Finally, we present a randomized extension of these algorithms that solves arbitrary (i.e., not necessarily homogeneous) instances of adaptive self-organization with high probability when nodes know the goal distributions.

ai.tldr: 提出在匿名动态网络中，节点需根据局部感知的环境信号自适应地改变颜色分布的问题，并给出了可解性条件及算法。
ai.motivation: 现有分布式自组织研究多假设网络拓扑稳定或节点可识别，而实际网络（如传感器网络、物联网）常匿名且拓扑动态变化，需要节点仅凭局部信息实现全局目标分布。
ai.method: 先证明确定性节点仅能解决同质目标分布实例，然后设计线性时间、对数空间的确定性算法，并给出已知节点数时的强收敛变体；最后提出随机化扩展以处理任意目标分布。
ai.result: 确定性算法可解决同质目标分布，随机化算法以高概率解决任意目标分布，且算法能抵御信号和拓扑的对抗性变化。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解在高度动态、匿名的分布式系统中如何实现自适应行为，为设计鲁棒的分布式控制协议提供理论基础。

idea_spark.idea: 利用随机化打破匿名性限制，使节点能通过概率性协调实现非均匀目标分布。
idea_spark.risk: 随机化算法可能以低概率失败，且需要节点已知目标分布，实际部署中分布可能未知或变化。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受生物群体自组织（如蚁群、蜂群）启发，将局部感知与全局目标结合，对抗环境扰动。

idea_spark.risk: 随机化算法可能以低概率失败，且需要节点已知目标分布，实际部署中分布可能未知或变化。

en.tldr: Introduces adaptive self-organization in anonymous dynamic networks where nodes must distributively change their color distribution based on local environmental signals, with solvability conditions and algorithms.
en.motivation: Existing distributed self-organization often assumes stable topology or identifiable nodes, but real networks (e.g., sensor networks, IoT) are anonymous and dynamic, requiring nodes to achieve global goals from local information.
en.method: First proves deterministic nodes can only solve homogeneous goal instances, then designs a linear-time, logarithmic-memory deterministic algorithm, with a stronger convergence variant when n is known; finally presents a randomized extension for arbitrary goal distributions.
en.result: Deterministic algorithm solves homogeneous goals; randomized algorithm solves arbitrary goals with high probability, both resilient to adversarial signal and topology changes.
en.help_to_user: Provides theoretical foundations for designing robust distributed control protocols in highly dynamic, anonymous systems.

idea_spark.idea: Use randomization to break anonymity limits, enabling nodes to achieve non-uniform target distributions via probabilistic coordination.
idea_spark.risk: Randomized algorithm may fail with low probability and requires nodes to know goal distributions, which may be unknown or change in practice.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by biological self-organization (e.g., ant colonies, bee swarms) combining local sensing with global objectives against environmental perturbations.

papers.title: ProMax: Exploring the Potential of LLM-derived Profiles with Distribution Shaping for Recommender Systems
papers.summary: The remarkable text understanding and generation capabilities of large language models (LLMs) have revitalized the field of general recommendation based on implicit user feedback. Rather than deploying LLMs directly as recommendation models, a more flexible paradigm leverages their ability to interpret users' historical interactions and semantic contexts to extract structured profiles that characterize user preferences. These profiles can be further transformed into actionable high-dimensional representations, serving as powerful signals to augment and strengthen recommendation models. However, the mechanism by which such profiles enhance recommendation performance within the feature space remains insufficiently understood. Moreover, existing studies predominantly rely on nonlinear alignment and fusion strategies to incorporate these profiles, which often lead to semantic loss and fail to fully exploit their potential. To address these limitations, we revisit profiles from a retrieval perspective and propose a simple yet effective recommendation framework built upon distribution shaping (ProMax) in this paper. We begin by employing dense retrieval to uncover the collaborative relationships between user and item profiles within the feature space. Based on this insight, we introduce a dual distribution-reshaping process, in which the profile distribution acts as a guiding signal to steer the recommendation model toward learning user preferences for unseen items beyond the scope of observed interactions. We apply ProMax to four classic recommendation methods on three public datasets. The results indicate that ProMax substantially improves base model performance and outperforms existing LLM-based recommendation approaches.

ai.tldr: 提出ProMax框架，利用大语言模型生成的用户画像，通过分布重塑增强推荐系统性能。
ai.motivation: 现有基于LLM画像的推荐方法在特征空间中理解不足，且非线性融合导致语义损失。
ai.method: 从检索视角重新审视画像，采用密集检索发现用户与物品画像的协作关系，并引入双重分布重塑过程。
ai.result: 在三个公开数据集上显著提升四种经典推荐方法，优于现有基于LLM的推荐方法。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解如何利用LLM画像通过分布重塑提升推荐效果。

idea_spark.idea: 将画像分布作为引导信号，使模型学习用户对未见物品的偏好。
idea_spark.risk: 分布重塑可能过度依赖画像质量，若画像不准确会引入噪声。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从检索视角处理画像，启发将LLM生成信息视为可检索的协作信号。

zh.tldr: 提出ProMax框架，利用大语言模型生成的用户画像，通过分布重塑增强推荐系统性能。
zh.motivation: 现有基于LLM画像的推荐方法在特征空间中理解不足，且非线性融合导致语义损失。
zh.method: 从检索视角重新审视画像，采用密集检索发现用户与物品画像的协作关系，并引入双重分布重塑过程。
zh.result: 在三个公开数据集上显著提升四种经典推荐方法，优于现有基于LLM的推荐方法。
zh.help_to_user: 帮助理解如何利用LLM画像通过分布重塑提升推荐效果。

idea_spark.idea: 将画像分布作为引导信号，使模型学习用户对未见物品的偏好。
idea_spark.risk: 分布重塑可能过度依赖画像质量，若画像不准确会引入噪声。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从检索视角处理画像，启发将LLM生成信息视为可检索的协作信号。

en.tldr: Proposes ProMax, a framework that leverages LLM-derived user profiles via distribution shaping to enhance recommender systems.
en.motivation: Existing LLM-profile-based recommendation methods lack understanding in feature space and suffer semantic loss from nonlinear fusion.
en.method: Revisits profiles from a retrieval perspective, uses dense retrieval to uncover collaborative relationships, and introduces a dual distribution-reshaping process.
en.result: Substantially improves four classic recommendation methods on three public datasets, outperforming existing LLM-based approaches.
en.help_to_user: Helps understand how to use LLM-derived profiles with distribution shaping to improve recommendation performance.

idea_spark.idea: Using profile distribution as a guiding signal to learn user preferences for unseen items.
idea_spark.risk: Over-reliance on profile quality; inaccurate profiles may introduce noise.
idea_spark.inspiration: Treating LLM-generated profiles as retrievable collaborative signals inspires new ways to integrate LLM outputs.

papers.title: Delineating Knowledge Boundaries for Honest Large Vision-Language Models
papers.summary: Large Vision-Language Models (VLMs) have achieved remarkable multimodal performance yet remain prone to factual hallucinations, particularly in long-tail or specialized domains. Moreover, current models exhibit a weak capacity to refuse queries that exceed their parametric knowledge. In this paper, we propose a systematic framework to enhance the refusal capability of VLMs when facing such unknown questions. We first curate a model-specific "Visual-Idk" (Visual-I don't know) dataset, leveraging multi-sample consistency probing to distinguish between known and unknown facts. We then align the model using supervised fine-tuning followed by preference-aware optimization (e.g., DPO, ORPO) to effectively delineate its knowledge boundaries. Results on the Visual-Idk dataset show our method improves the Truthful Rate from 57.9\% to 67.3\%. Additionally, internal probing also demonstrates that the model genuinely recognizes its boundaries instead of just memorizing refusal patterns. Our framework further generalizes to out-of-distribution medical and perceptual domains, providing a robust path toward more trustworthy and prudent visual assistants.

ai.tldr: 提出系统框架增强大型视觉语言模型对未知问题的拒绝能力，通过构建模型专属数据集和偏好优化对齐，将真实率从57.9%提升至67.3%，并泛化到医学和感知领域。
ai.motivation: 现有视觉语言模型在长尾或专业领域易产生事实性幻觉，且缺乏对超出参数知识问题的拒绝能力。
ai.method: 首先利用多样本一致性探测构建模型专属Visual-Idk数据集，区分已知与未知事实；然后通过监督微调和偏好感知优化（如DPO、ORPO）对齐模型，明确其知识边界。
ai.result: 在Visual-Idk数据集上真实率从57.9%提升至67.3%；内部探测表明模型真正识别边界而非记忆拒绝模式；方法泛化到分布外的医学和感知领域。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何构建更可信的视觉助手，减少幻觉并增强对未知问题的恰当拒绝。

idea_spark.idea: 利用模型内部一致性探测自动生成未知样本，结合偏好优化实现知识边界自感知。
idea_spark.risk: 可能过度拒绝导致可用性下降，需平衡拒绝与回答的阈值。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从模型自身的不确定性出发，而非依赖外部知识库，实现轻量级边界界定。

zh.tldr: 提出系统框架增强大型视觉语言模型对未知问题的拒绝能力，通过构建模型专属数据集和偏好优化对齐，将真实率从57.9%提升至67.3%，并泛化到医学和感知领域。

zh.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何构建更可信的视觉助手，减少幻觉并增强对未知问题的恰当拒绝。

idea_spark.idea: 利用模型内部一致性探测自动生成未知样本，结合偏好优化实现知识边界自感知。
idea_spark.risk: 可能过度拒绝导致可用性下降，需平衡拒绝与回答的阈值。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从模型自身的不确定性出发，而非依赖外部知识库，实现轻量级边界界定。

en.tldr: Proposes a systematic framework to enhance VLMs' refusal capability for unknown questions, building a model-specific Visual-Idk dataset via multi-sample consistency probing and aligning with preference-aware optimization, improving Truthful Rate from 57.9% to 67.3% and generalizing to medical and perceptual domains.
en.motivation: Current VLMs suffer from factual hallucinations in long-tail or specialized domains and lack the ability to refuse queries beyond their parametric knowledge.
en.method: First, curate a model-specific Visual-Idk dataset using multi-sample consistency probing to distinguish known from unknown facts; then align the model via supervised fine-tuning and preference-aware optimization (e.g., DPO, ORPO) to delineate knowledge boundaries.
en.result: Truthful Rate improved from 57.9% to 67.3% on Visual-Idk; internal probing shows genuine boundary recognition; method generalizes to out-of-distribution medical and perceptual domains.
en.help_to_user: Helps users build more trustworthy visual assistants by reducing hallucinations and enabling appropriate refusal of unknown questions.

idea_spark.idea: Automatically generate unknown samples using model internal consistency probing and combine with preference optimization for self-aware knowledge boundaries.
idea_spark.risk: May lead to over-refusal reducing usability; requires careful threshold balancing between refusal and answering.
idea_spark.inspiration: Leverage model's own uncertainty rather than external knowledge bases for lightweight boundary delineation.

papers.title: Hierarchical Multi-Persona Induction from User Behavioral Logs: Learning Evidence-Grounded and Truthful Personas
papers.summary: Behavioral logs provide rich signals for user modeling, but are noisy and interleaved across diverse intents. Recent work uses LLMs to generate interpretable natural-language personas from user logs, yet evaluation often emphasizes downstream utility, providing limited assurance of persona quality itself. We propose a hierarchical framework that aggregates user actions into intent memories and induces multiple evidence-grounded personas by clustering and labeling these memories. We formulate persona induction as an optimization problem over persona quality-captured by cluster cohesion, persona-evidence alignment, and persona truthfulness-and train the persona model using a groupwise extension of Direct Preference Optimization (DPO). Experiments on a large-scale service log and two public datasets show that our method induces more coherent, evidence-grounded, and trustworthy personas, while also improving future interaction prediction.

ai.tldr: 提出一种层次化框架，从用户行为日志中归纳多个人格，通过聚类和标签化意图记忆，并利用群组DPO优化人格质量。
ai.motivation: 现有方法依赖LLM生成人格，但评估偏重下游任务，缺乏对人格本身质量的保证。
ai.method: 将用户行为聚合为意图记忆，聚类并标注形成多个人格；将人格归纳建模为优化问题，使用群组DPO训练。
ai.result: 在大型服务日志和两个公开数据集上，方法生成更连贯、有证据支持且可信的人格，并提升未来交互预测。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何从行为日志中自动提取高质量、可解释的用户人格画像。

idea_spark.idea: 将人格归纳与证据对齐和真实性约束结合，类似方法可用于生成可解释的用户画像或行为摘要。
idea_spark.risk: 人格真实性依赖日志质量，噪声日志可能导致错误归纳；优化目标可能过度拟合特定质量指标。
idea_spark.inspiration: 群组DPO的扩展思路可启发其他多目标优化任务，如多任务学习中的偏好对齐。

en.tldr: Proposes a hierarchical framework to induce multiple personas from user behavioral logs by clustering intent memories and optimizing persona quality via groupwise DPO.
en.motivation: Existing LLM-based persona generation emphasizes downstream utility, lacking assurance of persona quality itself.
en.method: Aggregates user actions into intent memories, clusters and labels them to form personas; formulates persona induction as an optimization problem and trains with groupwise DPO.
en.result: On a large-scale service log and two public datasets, the method induces more coherent, evidence-grounded, and trustworthy personas, and improves future interaction prediction.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how to automatically extract high-quality, interpretable user personas from behavioral logs.

idea_spark.idea: Combining persona induction with evidence alignment and truthfulness constraints can be applied to generate interpretable user profiles or behavior summaries.
idea_spark.risk: Persona truthfulness depends on log quality; noisy logs may lead to incorrect induction; optimization may overfit specific quality metrics.
idea_spark.inspiration: The groupwise DPO extension can inspire other multi-objective optimization tasks, such as preference alignment in multi-task learning.

papers.title: Decoupled Prototype Matching with Vision Foundation Models for Few-Shot Industrial Object Detection
papers.summary: Industrial object detection systems typically rely on large annotated datasets, which are expensive to collect and challenging to maintain in industrial scenarios where the inventory of objects changes frequently. This work addresses the challenge of few-shot object detection in such industrial scenarios, where only a limited number of labeled samples are available for newly introduced objects. We present a detection framework that leverages vision foundation models to recognize objects with minimal supervision. The method constructs class prototypes from a small set of reference samples by extracting feature representations. For a given query scene during inference, object regions are generated using a segmentation model, and feature embeddings are extracted and matched with class prototypes using similarity matching. We evaluate the detection method on three established industrial datasets from the Benchmark for 6D Object Pose Estimation benchmark following the official 2D object detection evaluation protocol. We demonstrate competitive detection performance, improving AP by 6.9% compared to the state-of-the-art training-free detection methods. Furthermore, the presented method is able to onboard new objects using only a few reference images, without requiring any CAD models or large annotated datasets. These properties make the approach well-suited for real-world industrial applications.

ai.tldr: 提出一种基于视觉基础模型的解耦原型匹配方法，用于少样本工业目标检测，仅需少量参考图像即可识别新物体。
ai.motivation: 工业场景中目标种类频繁变化，收集大量标注数据成本高且难以维护，需要少样本检测方法。
ai.method: 利用视觉基础模型从少量参考样本提取特征构建类原型，推理时用分割模型生成候选区域，提取特征与原型进行相似度匹配。
ai.result: 在三个工业数据集上，AP比现有无训练方法提升6.9%，无需CAD模型或大量标注数据。
ai.help_to_user: 提供了一种在工业场景下快速部署新目标检测能力的方案，降低数据标注成本。

idea_spark.idea: 结合视觉基础模型与原型匹配，实现无需重新训练的增量式目标检测。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖分割模型质量，复杂背景或遮挡下候选区域可能不准确。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用基础模型的通用特征表示，通过原型匹配实现快速适应新类别，启发少样本学习新范式。

en.tldr: Proposes a decoupled prototype matching method using vision foundation models for few-shot industrial object detection, requiring only a few reference images to recognize new objects.
en.motivation: Industrial scenarios involve frequent changes in object inventory, making large annotated datasets expensive and hard to maintain, thus needing few-shot detection.
en.method: Extracts feature representations from few reference samples via vision foundation models to build class prototypes; during inference, generates object regions with a segmentation model and matches features to prototypes via similarity.
en.result: Achieves 6.9% AP improvement over state-of-the-art training-free methods on three industrial datasets, without CAD models or large annotations.
en.help_to_user: Provides a solution for rapidly deploying new object detection capabilities in industrial settings, reducing data annotation costs.

idea_spark.idea: Combines vision foundation models with prototype matching for incremental object detection without retraining.
idea_spark.risk: Relies on segmentation model quality; inaccurate proposals under complex backgrounds or occlusions may degrade performance.
idea_spark.inspiration: Leverages general feature representations from foundation models with prototype matching for fast adaptation to new classes, inspiring new few-shot learning paradigms.

papers.title: Naamah: A Large Scale Synthetic Sanskrit NER Corpus via DBpedia Seeding and LLM Generation
papers.summary: The digitisation of classical Sanskrit literature is impeded by a scarcity of annotated resources, particularly for Named Entity Recognition. While recent methodologies utilise generic Large Language Models (LLMs) for data augmentation, these approaches remain prone to error and often lack the reasoning depth required for classical grammar. In this work, we introduce Naamah, a high quality silver standard Sanskrit NER dataset comprising 102,942 sentences. We propose a methodology that combines entity extraction from DBpedia with the generative capabilities of a 24B parameter hybrid reasoning model to create grammatically natural and synthetically diverse training data. We utilize this dataset to benchmark two transformer architectures: the massive multilingual XLM RoBERTa and the parameter efficient IndicBERTv2.

ai.tldr: 提出Naamah，一个包含102,942句的高质量梵文命名实体识别数据集，通过DBpedia实体提取和24B参数混合推理模型生成。
ai.motivation: 梵文数字资源稀缺，现有LLM数据增强方法错误率高且缺乏古典语法推理深度。
ai.method: 结合DBpedia实体提取与24B参数混合推理模型，生成语法自然且多样化的训练数据。
ai.result: 构建了Naamah数据集，并用于评估XLM RoBERTa和IndicBERTv2两种Transformer架构。
ai.help_to_user: 提供可用于梵文NER研究的标注数据集和生成方法论。

idea_spark.idea: 利用混合推理模型增强语法正确性，减少生成错误。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖DBpedia覆盖度，可能遗漏特定领域实体。
idea_spark.inspiration: 结合知识图谱与LLM生成高质量标注数据，适用于资源匮乏语言。

en.tldr: Introduces Naamah, a high-quality silver-standard Sanskrit NER dataset of 102,942 sentences, created by combining DBpedia entity extraction with a 24B parameter hybrid reasoning model.
en.motivation: Scarcity of annotated Sanskrit resources; existing LLM-based augmentation suffers from errors and lacks classical grammar reasoning.
en.method: Entity extraction from DBpedia seeded with generative capabilities of a 24B hybrid reasoning model to produce grammatically natural and diverse training data.
en.result: Constructed Naamah dataset and benchmarked XLM RoBERTa and IndicBERTv2 transformer architectures.
en.help_to_user: Provides a ready-to-use NER dataset and a methodology for generating annotated data for low-resource classical languages.

idea_spark.idea: Use a hybrid reasoning model to enforce grammatical correctness and reduce generation errors.
idea_spark.risk: Dependence on DBpedia coverage may miss domain-specific entities.
idea_spark.inspiration: Combine knowledge graphs with LLMs to produce high-quality annotated data for resource-scarce languages.

papers.title: Factorized Latent Reasoning for LLM-based Recommendation
papers.summary: Large language models (LLMs) have recently been adopted for recommendation by framing user preference modeling as a language generation problem. However, existing latent reasoning approaches typically represent user intent with a single latent vector, which struggles to capture the inherently multi-faceted nature of user preferences. We propose Factorized Latent Reasoning (FLR), a novel framework for LLM-based sequential recommendation that decomposes latent reasoning into multiple disentangled preference factors. FLR introduces a lightweight multi-factor attention module that iteratively refines a latent thought representation, where each factor attends to distinct aspects of the user's interaction history. To encourage diversity and specialization, we design orthogonality, attention diversity, and sparsity regularization objectives, and dynamically aggregate factor contributions for the final prediction. We further integrate FLR with an efficient reinforcement learning strategy based on group-relative policy optimization, enabling stable alignment directly in the latent reasoning space. Experiments on multiple benchmarks show that FLR consistently outperforms strong baselines while improving robustness and interpretability.

ai.tldr: 提出因子化潜在推理框架，将用户意图分解为多个解耦的偏好因子，提升推荐性能与可解释性。
ai.motivation: 现有方法用单一潜在向量表示用户意图，难以捕捉用户偏好的多面性。
ai.method: 设计轻量多因子注意力模块，通过正交性、注意力多样性和稀疏性正则化迭代细化潜在思维表示，并结合组相对策略优化进行稳定对齐。
ai.result: 在多个基准上优于强基线，同时提升鲁棒性和可解释性。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解如何用LLM进行更精细的序列推荐建模。

idea_spark.idea: 将用户历史交互分解为多个独立偏好因子，每个因子关注不同方面。
idea_spark.risk: 因子数量需手动设定，可能影响泛化。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受多因子注意力机制和强化学习对齐启发。

en.motivation: Existing methods use a single latent vector for user intent, failing to capture multi-faceted preferences.

en.result: Outperforms strong baselines on multiple benchmarks with improved robustness and interpretability.
en.help_to_user: Helps understand how to model fine-grained sequential recommendations with LLMs.

idea_spark.idea: Decompose user interaction history into multiple independent preference factors, each attending to different aspects.
idea_spark.risk: The number of factors is manually set, which may affect generalization.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by multi-factor attention mechanisms and reinforcement learning alignment.

papers.title: HIVE: Hidden-Evidence Verification for Hallucination Detection in Diffusion Large Language Models
papers.summary: Diffusion large language models generate text through multi-step denoising, where hallucination signals may emerge throughout the trajectory rather than only in the final output. Existing detectors mainly rely on output uncertainty or coarse trace statistics, which often fail to capture the richer hidden dynamics of D-LLMs. We propose HIVE, a hidden-evidence verification framework that extracts compressed hidden evidence from denoising trajectories, selects informative step-layer evidence, and conditions a verifier language model on the selected evidence through prefix embeddings. HIVE produces both a continuous hallucination score from verifier decision logits and structured verification outputs, including hallucination types, evidence pairs, and short rationales. Across two D-LLMs and three QA benchmarks, HIVE consistently outperforms eight strong baselines and achieves up to 0.9236 AUROC and 0.9537 AUPRC. Ablation studies further confirm the importance of hidden-evidence conditioning, learned evidence selection, two-stream evidence representation, and step-layer embeddings. These results suggest that selected hidden evidence from denoising trajectories provides a stronger and more usable hallucination signal than output-only uncertainty or coarse trace statistics.

ai.tldr: 提出HIVE框架，通过提取扩散大语言模型去噪轨迹中的隐藏证据来检测幻觉，在多个基准上取得最优性能。
ai.motivation: 现有幻觉检测方法仅依赖输出不确定性或粗略轨迹统计，未能充分利用扩散模型去噪过程中的隐藏动态信息。
ai.method: 从去噪轨迹中提取压缩隐藏证据，选择信息丰富的步骤-层证据，通过前缀嵌入条件化验证器语言模型，输出连续幻觉分数和结构化验证结果。
ai.result: 在两种扩散大语言模型和三个问答基准上，HIVE一致优于八种强基线，AUROC最高达0.9236，AUPRC最高达0.9537。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户更准确地检测扩散大语言模型生成的文本中的幻觉，提高模型可靠性。

idea_spark.idea: 利用去噪轨迹中的中间隐藏状态作为证据，而非仅依赖最终输出，可能提升检测鲁棒性。
idea_spark.risk: 提取和选择隐藏证据可能增加计算开销，且对轨迹长度敏感。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受扩散模型多步去噪过程启发，类似思路可应用于其他需要细粒度中间信号的任务（如事实核查、异常检测）。

idea_spark.inspiration: 受扩散模型多步去噪过程启发，类似思路可应用于其他需要细粒度中间信号的任务（如事实核查、异常检测）。

en.tldr: Proposes HIVE, a framework that detects hallucinations in diffusion large language models by extracting hidden evidence from denoising trajectories, achieving state-of-the-art performance on multiple benchmarks.
en.motivation: Existing hallucination detectors rely on output uncertainty or coarse trace statistics, failing to capture richer hidden dynamics in diffusion models' denoising process.
en.method: Extracts compressed hidden evidence from denoising trajectories, selects informative step-layer evidence, and conditions a verifier language model via prefix embeddings, outputting continuous hallucination scores and structured verification results.
en.result: HIVE consistently outperforms eight strong baselines on two D-LLMs and three QA benchmarks, achieving up to 0.9236 AUROC and 0.9537 AUPRC.
en.help_to_user: Helps users more accurately detect hallucinations in text generated by diffusion large language models, improving model reliability.

idea_spark.idea: Leveraging intermediate hidden states from denoising trajectories as evidence, rather than only final outputs, may improve detection robustness.
idea_spark.risk: Extracting and selecting hidden evidence may increase computational cost and is sensitive to trajectory length.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the multi-step denoising process of diffusion models, similar ideas could apply to tasks requiring fine-grained intermediate signals (e.g., fact-checking, anomaly detection).

papers.title: Bridge: Basis-Driven Causal Inference Marries VFMs for Domain Generalization
papers.summary: Detectors often suffer from degraded performance, primarily due to the distributional gap between the source and target domains. This issue is especially evident in single-source domains with limited data, as models tend to rely on confounders (e.g., illumination, co-occurrence, and style) from the source domain, leading to spurious correlations that hinder generalization. To this end, this paper proposes a novel Basis-driven framework for domain generalization, namely \textbf{\textit{Bridge}}, that incorporates causal inference into object detection. By learning the low-rank bases for front-door adjustment, \textbf{\textit{Bridge}} blocks confounders' effects to mitigate spurious correlations, while simultaneously refining representations by filtering redundant and task-irrelevant components. \textbf{\textit{Bridge}} can be seamlessly integrated with both discriminative (e.g., DINOv2/3, SAM) and generative (e.g., Stable Diffusion) Vision Foundation Models (VFMs). Extensive experiments across multiple domain generalization object detection datasets, i.e., Cross-Camera, Adverse Weather, Real-to-Artistic, Diverse Weather Datasets, and Diverse Weather DroneVehicle (our newly augmented real-world UAV-based benchmark), underscore the superiority of our proposed method over previous state-of-the-art approaches. The project page is available at: https://mingbohong.github.io/Bridge/.

ai.tldr: 提出Bridge框架，通过因果推断中的前门调整和低秩基学习，消除混淆因素导致的虚假相关，提升目标检测在域泛化中的性能。
ai.motivation: 单源域数据有限时，模型易依赖光照、共现、风格等混淆因素，产生虚假相关，阻碍泛化。
ai.method: 学习低秩基进行前门调整，阻断混淆因素影响，同时过滤冗余和任务无关成分以精炼表征。
ai.result: 在跨相机、恶劣天气、真实到艺术、多样天气及新增无人机数据集上超越现有方法。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种可无缝集成判别式（DINOv2/3、SAM）和生成式（Stable Diffusion）视觉基础模型的域泛化检测框架。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将低秩基学习与扩散模型的生成先验结合，在推理时动态调整表征以适配未知域。
idea_spark.risk: 低秩假设可能不适用于高度非线性的混淆结构；前门调整需满足中介变量可观测性假设。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受因果推断中前门调整启发，将混淆因素视为不可观测变量，用低秩基近似中介路径。

zh.tldr: 提出Bridge框架，通过因果推断中的前门调整和低秩基学习，消除混淆因素导致的虚假相关，提升目标检测在域泛化中的性能。

zh.method: 学习低秩基进行前门调整，阻断混淆因素影响，同时过滤冗余和任务无关成分以精炼表征。
zh.result: 在跨相机、恶劣天气、真实到艺术、多样天气及新增无人机数据集上超越现有方法。
zh.help_to_user: 提供一种可无缝集成判别式（DINOv2/3、SAM）和生成式（Stable Diffusion）视觉基础模型的域泛化检测框架。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将低秩基学习与扩散模型的生成先验结合，在推理时动态调整表征以适配未知域。
idea_spark.risk: 低秩假设可能不适用于高度非线性的混淆结构；前门调整需满足中介变量可观测性假设。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受因果推断中前门调整启发，将混淆因素视为不可观测变量，用低秩基近似中介路径。

en.tldr: Proposes Bridge, a basis-driven causal inference framework for domain generalization in object detection, using front-door adjustment with low-rank bases to block confounders and refine representations.
en.motivation: Limited single-source domain data causes detectors to rely on confounders (e.g., illumination, co-occurrence, style), leading to spurious correlations that hinder generalization.
en.method: Learns low-rank bases for front-door adjustment to block confounder effects, while filtering redundant and task-irrelevant components to refine representations.
en.result: Outperforms state-of-the-art on multiple domain generalization object detection benchmarks, including cross-camera, adverse weather, real-to-artistic, diverse weather, and a new UAV dataset.
en.help_to_user: Offers a plug-and-play framework that integrates with both discriminative (DINOv2/3, SAM) and generative (Stable Diffusion) Vision Foundation Models for robust domain generalization.

idea_spark.idea: Explore combining low-rank basis learning with diffusion model generative priors to dynamically adapt representations to unseen domains at inference time.
idea_spark.risk: Low-rank assumption may fail for highly nonlinear confounding structures; front-door adjustment requires observability of mediators.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by front-door adjustment in causal inference, treats confounders as unobserved variables and uses low-rank bases to approximate mediating paths.

papers.title: Shorthand for Thought: Compressing LLM Reasoning via Entropy-Guided Supertokens
papers.summary: Reasoning in Large Language Models incurs significant inference-time compute, yet the token-level information structure of reasoning traces remains underexplored. We observe that reasoning tokens split into two functional types: low-entropy \textit{structural} tokens (recurring phrases that scaffold the reasoning process) and higher-entropy \textit{organic} tokens (problem-specific content that drives toward a solution). This asymmetry motivates a simple, model-agnostic compression pipeline: apply cross-word BPE merges on a model's own reasoning traces to derive \textit{supertokens} that capture frequent structural patterns, then teach the model to adopt them via supervised fine-tuning. Across three model families and five mathematical reasoning benchmarks, our approach shortens reasoning traces by 8.1\% on average with no statistically significant accuracy loss on any model--benchmark pair. Beyond compression, supertokens act as interpretable reasoning-move annotations (backtracking, verification, strategy shifts), exposing the model's high-level strategy at a glance. Analyzing transitions between structural categories reveals systematic differences between correct and incorrect traces: correct traces show productive recovery (backtracking followed by strategy shifts and verification), while incorrect traces are dominated by confusion cycles (repeated hedging and unresolved contradictions). These diagnostic signals suggest applications in reward shaping and early stopping for RL-based reasoning training.

ai.tldr: 提出一种基于熵引导的超令牌压缩方法，将推理轨迹中的结构令牌与有机令牌分离，通过跨词BPE合并和微调实现推理长度压缩8.1%且无显著精度损失，并揭示正确与错误推理轨迹的结构差异。
ai.motivation: 大语言模型推理时计算开销大，但推理轨迹的令牌级信息结构未被充分探索，存在低熵结构令牌和高熵有机令牌的不对称性。
ai.method: 对模型自身推理轨迹应用跨词BPE合并，提取频繁结构模式作为超令牌，并通过监督微调让模型采用这些超令牌。
ai.result: 在三个模型家族和五个数学推理基准上，平均缩短推理轨迹8.1%，无显著精度损失；超令牌可作为可解释的推理动作注释，正确轨迹显示生产性恢复，错误轨迹以混淆循环为主。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解LLM推理过程的结构，提供压缩推理轨迹的方法，并诊断推理错误模式。

idea_spark.idea: 利用超令牌的转移模式作为奖励信号，在强化学习推理训练中引导正确推理路径。
idea_spark.risk: 超令牌可能过度泛化，导致模型丢失问题特定细节；跨模型泛化性需验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从语言学中的功能词与实义词区分获得启发，将类似思想应用于模型内部表示分析。

idea_spark.risk: 超令牌可能过度泛化，导致模型丢失问题特定细节；跨模型泛化性需验证。

en.tldr: Proposes an entropy-guided supertoken compression method that separates structural and organic tokens in reasoning traces, achieving 8.1% trace length reduction without significant accuracy loss via cross-word BPE merges and fine-tuning, and reveals structural differences between correct and incorrect traces.
en.motivation: Reasoning in LLMs incurs high inference cost, but the token-level information structure of reasoning traces is underexplored, revealing an asymmetry between low-entropy structural tokens and higher-entropy organic tokens.
en.method: Apply cross-word BPE merges on the model's own reasoning traces to derive supertokens capturing frequent structural patterns, then teach the model to adopt them via supervised fine-tuning.
en.result: Across three model families and five math reasoning benchmarks, reasoning traces are shortened by 8.1% on average with no statistically significant accuracy loss; supertokens act as interpretable reasoning-move annotations, with correct traces showing productive recovery and incorrect traces dominated by confusion cycles.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand the structure of LLM reasoning processes, provides a method to compress reasoning traces, and diagnoses error patterns in reasoning.

idea_spark.idea: Use supertoken transition patterns as reward signals in reinforcement learning for reasoning training to guide correct reasoning paths.
idea_spark.risk: Supertokens may over-generalize, causing loss of problem-specific details; cross-model generalization needs verification.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the distinction between function words and content words in linguistics, applying similar ideas to internal model representation analysis.

papers.title: AirZoo: A Unified Large-Scale Dataset for Grounding Aerial Geometric 3D Vision
papers.summary: Despite the rapid progress in data-driven 3D vision, aerial geometric 3D vision remains a formidable challenge due to the severe scarcity of large-scale, high-fidelity training data. Existing benchmarks, predominantly biased toward ground-level or object-centric views, do not account for complex viewpoint transformations and diverse environmental conditions in UAV-based sensing. To bridge this critical gap, we propose AirZoo, a unified large-scale dataset and benchmark for grounding aerial geometric 3D vision. AirZoo possesses three appealing properties: 1) Scalable Generation Pipeline: Leveraging freely available, world-scale photogrammetric 3D meshes, it renders vast outdoor environments with customizable UAV flight trajectories and configurable weather/illumination. 2) Comprehensive Scene Diversity: It provides the most extensive coverage of region types to date (spanning 378 regions across 22 countries), systematically encompassing both highly structured urban landscapes and complex unstructured natural environments. 3) Rich Geometric Annotations: Each frame provides synchronized, pixel-level metric depth and precise 6-DoF geo-referenced poses, essential for geometry-aware learning. Through three rigorous evaluation tracks -- aerial image retrieval, cross-view matching, and multi-view 3D reconstruction -- we demonstrate that AirZoo serves as a powerful pre-training engine. Extensive experiments on both public and newly collected real-world benchmarks reveal that fine-tuning on AirZoo yields substantial performance gains for SoTA models (e.g., MegaLoc, RoMa, VGGT, and Depth Anything 3), establishing a new performance upper bound for aerial spatial intelligence.

ai.tldr: 提出AirZoo，一个大规模、高保真的航拍几何3D视觉数据集，覆盖22国378个区域，提供像素级深度和6自由度位姿标注，在多个任务上显著提升现有模型性能。
ai.motivation: 现有航拍3D视觉数据集规模小、保真度低，且多偏向地面或物体中心视角，无法满足无人机复杂视角和环境变化的需求。
ai.method: 利用全球可用的摄影测量3D网格，通过可定制的无人机飞行轨迹和可配置天气/光照渲染生成大规模数据，并同步提供像素级深度和6自由度地理参考位姿。
ai.result: 在航拍图像检索、跨视角匹配和多视图3D重建三个评测任务中，基于AirZoo微调的模型（如MegaLoc、RoMa、VGGT、Depth Anything 3）在公开和自采真实基准上均取得显著性能提升，达到新的上界。
ai.help_to_user: 提供统一的航拍几何3D视觉训练和评测基准，帮助研究者快速获取高质量数据，推动无人机空间智能发展。

idea_spark.idea: 利用AirZoo的多样场景和标注，设计跨域泛化的航拍3D重建模型，或结合时序信息进行动态场景理解。
idea_spark.risk: 数据生成依赖摄影测量网格质量，可能引入渲染伪影；真实场景与合成数据间的域差异可能限制泛化。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受AirZoo启发，可探索将类似生成策略用于其他稀缺数据领域，如水下或夜间3D视觉。

en.tldr: Proposes AirZoo, a large-scale high-fidelity aerial geometric 3D vision dataset covering 378 regions across 22 countries, with pixel-level depth and 6-DoF poses, boosting SoTA models on multiple tasks.
en.motivation: Existing aerial 3D vision datasets are small-scale and low-fidelity, biased toward ground-level or object-centric views, failing to address complex viewpoint transformations and environmental conditions in UAV sensing.
en.method: Leverages freely available world-scale photogrammetric 3D meshes to render vast outdoor environments with customizable UAV trajectories and configurable weather/illumination, providing synchronized pixel-level metric depth and precise 6-DoF geo-referenced poses.
en.result: Fine-tuning on AirZoo yields substantial performance gains for SoTA models (e.g., MegaLoc, RoMa, VGGT, Depth Anything 3) on aerial image retrieval, cross-view matching, and multi-view 3D reconstruction, establishing a new upper bound.
en.help_to_user: Provides a unified training and evaluation benchmark for aerial geometric 3D vision, enabling researchers to access high-quality data and advance UAV spatial intelligence.

idea_spark.idea: Leverage AirZoo's diverse scenes and annotations to design cross-domain generalizable aerial 3D reconstruction models, or incorporate temporal information for dynamic scene understanding.
idea_spark.risk: Data generation depends on photogrammetric mesh quality, which may introduce rendering artifacts; domain gap between synthetic and real data could limit generalization.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by AirZoo, explore similar generation strategies for other data-scarce domains like underwater or nighttime 3D vision.

papers.title: A New Semisupervised Technique for Polarity Analysis using Masked Language Models
papers.summary: I developed a new version of Latent Semantic Scaling (LSS) employing word2vec as a masked language model. Unlike original spatial models, it assigns polarity scores to words and documents as predicted probabilities of seed words to occur in given contexts. These probabilistic polarity scores are more accurate, interpretable and consistent than those spatial polarity models can produce in text analysis. I demonstrate these advantages by applying both probabilistic and spatial models to China Daily's coverage of China and other countries during the coronavirus disease (COVID) pandemic in terms of achievement in health issues. The result suggests that more advanced masked language models would further improve the semisupervised machine learning technique.

ai.tldr: 提出一种基于掩码语言模型（word2vec）的新半监督极性分析方法，通过概率极性分数提高文本分析的准确性、可解释性和一致性。
ai.motivation: 传统空间极性模型（如原始LSS）在文本分析中准确性和可解释性有限，需要更可靠的半监督技术。
ai.method: 将word2vec作为掩码语言模型，计算词语和文档的极性分数为种子词在给定上下文中出现的预测概率。
ai.result: 概率极性分数比空间模型更准确、可解释且一致；应用于《中国日报》COVID-19报道，验证了优势。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种改进的半监督极性分析工具，适用于情感分析、舆论监测等文本分析任务。

idea_spark.idea: 探索更先进的掩码语言模型（如BERT）替代word2vec，进一步提升性能。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖高质量种子词选择，且计算成本可能随模型复杂度增加。
idea_spark.inspiration: 概率框架可推广至其他文本分类任务，如主题检测或立场分析。

zh.motivation: 传统空间极性模型（如原始LSS）在文本分析中准确性和可解释性有限，需要更可靠的半监督技术。

idea_spark.idea: 探索更先进的掩码语言模型（如BERT）替代word2vec，进一步提升性能。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖高质量种子词选择，且计算成本可能随模型复杂度增加。
idea_spark.inspiration: 概率框架可推广至其他文本分类任务，如主题检测或立场分析。

en.tldr: Proposes a new semisupervised technique for polarity analysis using word2vec as a masked language model, yielding probabilistic polarity scores that are more accurate, interpretable, and consistent than spatial models.
en.motivation: Traditional spatial polarity models (e.g., original LSS) suffer from limited accuracy and interpretability in text analysis, necessitating a more reliable semisupervised approach.
en.method: Employs word2vec as a masked language model to compute polarity scores as predicted probabilities of seed words occurring in given contexts for both words and documents.
en.result: Probabilistic polarity scores outperform spatial models in accuracy, interpretability, and consistency; demonstrated on China Daily's COVID-19 coverage.
en.help_to_user: Provides an improved semisupervised polarity analysis tool applicable to sentiment analysis, opinion monitoring, and other text analysis tasks.

idea_spark.idea: Explore more advanced masked language models (e.g., BERT) to replace word2vec for further performance gains.
idea_spark.risk: Relies on high-quality seed word selection and may incur higher computational cost with complex models.
idea_spark.inspiration: The probabilistic framework can be generalized to other text classification tasks, such as topic detection or stance analysis.

papers.title: RADIO-ViPE: Online Tightly Coupled Multi-Modal Fusion for Open-Vocabulary Semantic SLAM in Dynamic Environments
papers.summary: We present RADIO-ViPE (Reduce All Domains Into One -- Video Pose Engine), an online semantic SLAM system that enables geometry-aware open-vocabulary grounding, associating arbitrary natural language queries with localized 3D regions and objects in dynamic environments. Unlike existing approaches that require calibrated, posed RGB-D input, RADIO-ViPE operates directly on raw monocular RGB video streams, requiring no prior camera intrinsics, depth sensors, or pose initialization. The system tightly couples multi-modal embeddings -- spanning vision and language -- derived from agglomerative foundation models (e.g., RADIO) with geometric scene information. This coupling takes place in initialization, optimization and factor graph connections to improve the consistency of the map from multiple modalities. The optimization is wrapped within adaptive robust kernels, designed to handle both actively moving objects and agent-displaced scene elements (e.g., furniture rearranged during ego-centric session). Experiments demonstrate that RADIO-ViPE achieves state-of-the-art results on the dynamic TUM-RGBD benchmark while maintaining competitive performance against offline open-vocabulary methods that rely on calibrated data and static scene assumptions. RADIO-ViPE bridges a critical gap in real-world deployment, enabling robust open-vocabulary semantic grounding for autonomous robotics and unconstrained in-the-wild video streams. Project page: https://be2rlab.github.io/radio_vipe

ai.tldr: 提出RADIO-ViPE系统，在动态环境中通过单目RGB视频实现开放词汇语义SLAM，无需标定或深度传感器。
ai.motivation: 现有方法依赖标定RGB-D输入和静态场景假设，难以部署于真实动态环境。
ai.method: 紧密耦合视觉-语言多模态嵌入与几何信息，在初始化、优化和因子图连接中融合，并采用自适应鲁棒核处理动态物体。
ai.result: 在动态TUM-RGBD基准上达到最先进水平，与依赖标定数据的离线方法性能相当。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何在不依赖深度传感器和标定的情况下，从单目视频流中实时构建语义地图并理解自然语言查询。

idea_spark.idea: 利用基础模型（如RADIO）的通用嵌入替代传统特征，实现零样本语义定位。
idea_spark.risk: 对极端动态或低纹理场景的鲁棒性可能不足。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将视觉-语言预训练模型直接嵌入SLAM优化循环，而非仅作为后处理模块。

zh.tldr: 提出RADIO-ViPE系统，在动态环境中通过单目RGB视频实现开放词汇语义SLAM，无需标定或深度传感器。
zh.motivation: 现有方法依赖标定RGB-D输入和静态场景假设，难以部署于真实动态环境。
zh.method: 紧密耦合视觉-语言多模态嵌入与几何信息，在初始化、优化和因子图连接中融合，并采用自适应鲁棒核处理动态物体。
zh.result: 在动态TUM-RGBD基准上达到最先进水平，与依赖标定数据的离线方法性能相当。
zh.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何在不依赖深度传感器和标定的情况下，从单目视频流中实时构建语义地图并理解自然语言查询。

idea_spark.idea: 利用基础模型（如RADIO）的通用嵌入替代传统特征，实现零样本语义定位。
idea_spark.risk: 对极端动态或低纹理场景的鲁棒性可能不足。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将视觉-语言预训练模型直接嵌入SLAM优化循环，而非仅作为后处理模块。

en.tldr: RADIO-ViPE is an online semantic SLAM system that performs open-vocabulary grounding from monocular RGB video in dynamic environments without calibration or depth sensors.
en.motivation: Existing methods require calibrated RGB-D input and static scene assumptions, limiting real-world deployment.
en.method: Tightly couples vision-language embeddings from foundation models with geometric information via initialization, optimization, and factor graph connections, using adaptive robust kernels to handle moving objects.
en.result: Achieves state-of-the-art on dynamic TUM-RGBD benchmark, competitive with offline methods relying on calibrated data.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how to build semantic maps and interpret natural language queries from monocular video streams without depth sensors or calibration.

idea_spark.idea: Leverage generic embeddings from foundation models (e.g., RADIO) to replace traditional features for zero-shot semantic localization.
idea_spark.risk: May struggle in extreme dynamic or low-texture scenes.
idea_spark.inspiration: Embed vision-language pretrained models directly into the SLAM optimization loop rather than using them as a post-processing module.

papers.title: LLMs Generate Kitsch
papers.summary: Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly used to generate pictures, texts, music, videos, and other works that have traditionally required human creativity. LLM-generated artifacts are often rated better than human-generated works in controlled studies. At the same time, they can come across as generic and hollow. We propose to resolve this tension by arguing that LLMs systematically generate kitsch, and that this is a consequence of the way in which they are trained. We also show empirically that readers perceive LLM-generated stories as kitschier, if we control for their definition of "kitsch". We discuss implications for the design of future studies and for creative tasks such as research and coding.

ai.tldr: 大型语言模型（LLM）生成的作品常被评价为比人类作品更好，但同时也显得空洞和俗套。本文提出LLM系统性地生成“媚俗”（kitsch），并证明读者在控制定义后确实认为LLM故事更媚俗。
ai.motivation: 解释LLM生成作品在质量评价中表现优异却给人空洞感这一矛盾现象。
ai.method: 通过理论论证和实证研究，控制“媚俗”定义后让读者评价LLM生成的故事。
ai.result: 读者认为LLM生成的故事更媚俗，且这一现象源于LLM的训练方式。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解LLM生成内容的本质缺陷，避免盲目依赖其创意输出。

idea_spark.risk: 过度强调“媚俗”可能忽视LLM在特定任务中的实用价值。

zh.tldr: 大型语言模型（LLM）生成的作品常被评价为比人类作品更好，但同时也显得空洞和俗套。本文提出LLM系统性地生成“媚俗”（kitsch），并证明读者在控制定义后确实认为LLM故事更媚俗。
zh.motivation: 解释LLM生成作品在质量评价中表现优异却给人空洞感这一矛盾现象。
zh.method: 通过理论论证和实证研究，控制“媚俗”定义后让读者评价LLM生成的故事。
zh.result: 读者认为LLM生成的故事更媚俗，且这一现象源于LLM的训练方式。
zh.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解LLM生成内容的本质缺陷，避免盲目依赖其创意输出。

idea_spark.idea: 设计实验量化LLM生成内容中“媚俗”的具体特征（如重复模式、情感浅薄）。
idea_spark.risk: 过度强调“媚俗”可能忽视LLM在特定任务中的实用价值。

en.tldr: LLMs generate artifacts that are often rated better than human works but feel generic and hollow. This paper argues they systematically produce kitsch, and shows empirically that readers perceive LLM stories as kitschier when controlling for the definition of 'kitsch'.
en.motivation: To resolve the tension between high ratings and perceived hollowness of LLM-generated works.
en.method: Theoretical argument combined with an empirical study where readers rated LLM-generated stories for kitschiness under a controlled definition.
en.result: Readers perceive LLM stories as kitschier, and this stems from the training process of LLMs.
en.help_to_user: Helps users recognize the inherent limitations of LLM-generated content for creative tasks.

idea_spark.idea: Design experiments to quantify specific kitsch features (e.g., repetitive patterns, shallow emotion) in LLM outputs.
idea_spark.risk: Overemphasizing kitsch may overlook practical utility of LLMs in certain tasks.
idea_spark.inspiration: Borrowing from art theory to establish new evaluation dimensions for AI-generated content.

papers.title: Quantum Gatekeeper: Multi-Factor Context-Bound Image Steganography with VQC Based Key Derivation on Quantum Hardware
papers.summary: This paper presents Quantum Gatekeeper, a context-bound image steganography framework where successful payload recovery depends on both cryptographic decryption and the reconstruction of a precise extraction path. The system integrates lossless least significant bit (LSB) embedding with a deterministic variational quantum circuit (VQC)-derived gate key, multi-factor contextual binding, and authenticated encryption. Payload extraction is contingent upon four requisite factors: a password, a shared secret, a user-supplied context string, and a reference image signature. Any deviation in these factors causes the system to read from an incorrect pixel sequence or fail authentication, resulting in silent rejection rather than partial disclosure. The proposed method derives a gatecontrolled extraction key from a seed-conditioned variational circuit, with parameters generated via cryptographic hash expansion and context-dependent image features. To ensure encode/decode consistency, the cryptographic key path is generated via exact statevector simulation; concurrently, IBM superconducting quantum hardware is utilized to evaluate the statistical behavior of the circuit family under physical noise. We introduce a dual-region image layout to resolve the nonce bootstrapping dependency, separating header recovery from payload recovery through independently derived keys. Experimental results confirm successful end-to-end message embedding and recovery on PNG images, demonstrating deterministic success under correct conditions and failure otherwise. The framework supports both text and image payloads; in the image-in-image configuration, a secret image is resized to a fixed resolution prior to embedding, enabling exact pixel-level recovery under correct contextual reconstruction.

ai.tldr: 提出一种基于变分量子电路的上下文绑定图像隐写框架，有效载荷恢复需同时满足密码解密和精确提取路径重建。
ai.motivation: 传统隐写术缺乏对提取路径的强约束，易被部分破解。
ai.method: 结合无损LSB嵌入、确定性VQC密钥派生、多因素上下文绑定和认证加密，提取需密码、共享秘密、上下文串和参考图像签名四因素。
ai.result: 在PNG图像上实现端到端嵌入与恢复，正确条件下确定性成功，否则静默拒绝。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种高安全性的图像隐写方案，适用于需要严格访问控制的场景。

idea_spark.idea: 利用量子电路的噪声特性作为额外安全层，使密钥对物理环境敏感。
idea_spark.risk: 量子硬件噪声可能导致密钥不一致，需精确状态向量模拟保证确定性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将量子计算的不可预测性转化为安全优势，类似物理不可克隆函数。

en.tldr: A context-bound image steganography framework using variational quantum circuits for key derivation, requiring both cryptographic decryption and precise extraction path reconstruction for payload recovery.
en.motivation: Traditional steganography lacks strong constraints on extraction paths, making it vulnerable to partial compromise.
en.method: Combines lossless LSB embedding, deterministic VQC-derived gate key, multi-factor contextual binding, and authenticated encryption; extraction requires password, shared secret, context string, and reference image signature.
en.result: End-to-end embedding and recovery on PNG images with deterministic success under correct conditions and silent rejection otherwise.
en.help_to_user: Provides a high-security image steganography scheme suitable for scenarios requiring strict access control.

idea_spark.idea: Leverage quantum hardware noise as an additional security layer, making keys sensitive to physical environment.
idea_spark.risk: Quantum hardware noise may cause key inconsistency; exact statevector simulation is needed to ensure determinism.
idea_spark.inspiration: Turn unpredictability of quantum computing into a security advantage, similar to physical unclonable functions.

papers.title: BioGraphletQA: Knowledge-Anchored Generation of Complex QA Datasets
papers.summary: This paper presents a principled and scalable framework for systematically generating complex Question Answering (QA) data. In the core of this framework is a graphlet-anchored generation process, where small subgraphs from a Knowledge Graph (KG) are used in a structured prompt to control the complexity and ensure the factual grounding of questions generated by Large Language Models. The first instantiation of this framework is BioGraphletQA, a new biomedical KGQA dataset of 119,856 QA pairs. Each entry is grounded in a graphlet of up to five nodes from the OREGANO KG, with most of the pairs being enriched with relevant document snippets from PubMed. We start by demonstrating the framework's value and the dataset's quality through evaluation by a domain expert on 106 QA pairs, confirming the high scientific validity and complexity of the generated data. Secondly, we establish its practical utility by showing that augmenting downstream benchmarks with our data improves accuracy on PubMedQA from 49.2% to 68.5% in a low-resource setting, and on MedQA from a 41.4% baseline to 44.8% in a full-resource setting. Our framework provides a robust and generalizable solution for creating critical resources to advance complex QA tasks, including MCQA and KGQA. All resources supporting this work, including the dataset (https://zenodo.org/records/17381119) and framework code (https://github.com/ieeta-pt/BioGraphletQA), are publicly available to facilitate use, reproducibility and extension.
authors: Bárbara Maria Ribeiro de Abreu Martins

ai.tldr: 提出一个基于知识图谱子图锚定的复杂问答数据集生成框架，并构建了生物医学领域包含119,856个问答对的BioGraphletQA数据集。
ai.motivation: 现有复杂问答数据集生成缺乏可扩展性和事实准确性，且难以控制问题复杂度。
ai.method: 利用知识图谱中的小规模子图（graphlet）作为结构化提示，引导大语言模型生成事实准确且复杂度可控的问答对。
ai.result: 专家评估106个问答对确认高科学有效性和复杂度；在低资源设置下PubMedQA准确率从49.2%提升至68.5%，MedQA从41.4%提升至44.8%。
ai.help_to_user: 提供现成的复杂问答数据集和可复现的生成框架，可用于训练和评估生物医学问答模型。

idea_spark.idea: 利用知识图谱子图锚定生成多跳推理问题，并自动关联相关文献片段。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖知识图谱的完整性和质量，子图选择策略可能引入偏差。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将结构化知识图谱与语言模型生成能力结合，实现可控且可扩展的数据合成。

idea_spark.risk: 依赖知识图谱的完整性和质量，子图选择策略可能引入偏差。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将结构化知识图谱与语言模型生成能力结合，实现可控且可扩展的数据合成。

en.tldr: Proposes a principled and scalable framework for generating complex QA datasets anchored by knowledge graph graphlets, and constructs BioGraphletQA with 119,856 biomedical QA pairs.
en.motivation: Existing complex QA dataset generation lacks scalability and factual grounding, and struggles to control question complexity.
en.method: Uses small subgraphs (graphlets) from a knowledge graph as structured prompts to guide LLMs in generating factually grounded QA pairs with controlled complexity.
en.result: Expert evaluation of 106 QA pairs confirms high scientific validity and complexity; augmenting low-resource PubMedQA improves accuracy from 49.2% to 68.5%, and MedQA from 41.4% to 44.8%.
en.help_to_user: Provides a ready-to-use complex QA dataset and a reproducible generation framework for training and evaluating biomedical QA models.

idea_spark.idea: Leverage knowledge graph graphlets to generate multi-hop reasoning questions and automatically link relevant document snippets.
idea_spark.risk: Dependence on KG completeness and quality; graphlet selection strategy may introduce bias.
idea_spark.inspiration: Combine structured knowledge graphs with LLM generation for controllable and scalable data synthesis.

papers.title: OCR-Memory: Optical Context Retrieval for Long-Horizon Agent Memory
papers.summary: Autonomous LLM agents increasingly operate in long-horizon, interactive settings where success depends on reusing experience accumulated over extended histories. However, existing agent memory systems are fundamentally constrained by text-context budgets: storing or revisiting raw trajectories is prohibitively token-expensive, while summarization and text-only retrieval trade token savings for information loss and fragmented evidence. To address this limitation, we propose Optical Context Retrieval Memory (OCR-Memory), a memory framework that leverages the visual modality as a high-density representation of agent experience, enabling retention of arbitrarily long histories with minimal prompt overhead at retrieval time. Specifically, OCR-Memory renders historical trajectories into images annotated with unique visual identifiers. OCR-Memory retrieves stored experience via a \emph{locate-and-transcribe} paradigm that selects relevant regions through visual anchors and retrieves the corresponding verbatim text, avoiding free-form generation and reducing hallucination. Experiments on long-horizon agent benchmarks show consistent gains under strict context limits, demonstrating that optical encoding increases effective memory capacity while preserving faithful evidence recovery.

ai.tldr: 提出OCR-Memory，利用视觉模态作为高密度表示，通过定位-转录范式检索历史轨迹，在长时程智能体任务中提升记忆容量并减少幻觉。
ai.motivation: 现有智能体记忆系统受限于文本上下文预算，存储原始轨迹代价高昂，摘要和纯文本检索导致信息丢失和证据碎片化。
ai.method: 将历史轨迹渲染为带唯一视觉标识的图像，通过视觉锚点定位相关区域，再转录对应文本，避免自由生成。
ai.result: 在长时程智能体基准测试中，严格上下文限制下持续取得性能提升，光学编码增加了有效记忆容量并保持证据忠实恢复。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户构建能高效利用长历史经验的LLM智能体，减少token消耗和幻觉。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将多模态编码（如音频、触觉）与视觉标识结合，进一步压缩记忆表示。
idea_spark.risk: 视觉渲染可能引入额外计算开销，且对复杂动态场景的适应性需验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受人类视觉记忆和空间锚定启发，将抽象文本经验转化为可定位的视觉场景。

zh.tldr: 提出OCR-Memory，利用视觉模态作为高密度表示，通过定位-转录范式检索历史轨迹，在长时程智能体任务中提升记忆容量并减少幻觉。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将多模态编码（如音频、触觉）与视觉标识结合，进一步压缩记忆表示。
idea_spark.risk: 视觉渲染可能引入额外计算开销，且对复杂动态场景的适应性需验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受人类视觉记忆和空间锚定启发，将抽象文本经验转化为可定位的视觉场景。

en.tldr: Proposes OCR-Memory, which uses the visual modality as a high-density representation and retrieves historical trajectories via a locate-and-transcribe paradigm, improving memory capacity and reducing hallucination in long-horizon agent tasks.
en.motivation: Existing agent memory systems are constrained by text-context budgets; storing raw trajectories is token-expensive, while summarization and text-only retrieval cause information loss and fragmented evidence.
en.method: Renders historical trajectories into images annotated with unique visual identifiers, selects relevant regions via visual anchors, and retrieves verbatim text to avoid free-form generation.
en.result: Consistent gains on long-horizon agent benchmarks under strict context limits; optical encoding increases effective memory capacity while preserving faithful evidence recovery.
en.help_to_user: Helps users build LLM agents that efficiently leverage long historical experience, reducing token consumption and hallucination.

idea_spark.idea: Explore combining multimodal encoding (e.g., audio, tactile) with visual identifiers to further compress memory representation.
idea_spark.risk: Visual rendering may introduce additional computational overhead, and adaptability to complex dynamic scenes needs verification.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by human visual memory and spatial anchoring, transforming abstract textual experience into locatable visual scenes.

papers.title: Star-Fusion: A Multi-modal Transformer Architecture for Discrete Celestial Orientation via Spherical Topology
papers.summary: Reliable celestial attitude determination is a critical requirement for autonomous spacecraft navigation, yet traditional "Lost-in-Space" (LIS) algorithms often suffer from high computational overhead and sensitivity to sensor-induced noise. While deep learning has emerged as a promising alternative, standard regression models are often confounded by the non-Euclidean topology of the celestial sphere and by the periodic boundary conditions of Right Ascension (RA) and Declination (Dec). In this paper, we present Star-Fusion, a multi-modal architecture that reformulates orientation estimation as a discrete topological classification task. Our approach leverages spherical K-Means clustering to partition the celestial sphere into K topologically consistent regions, effectively mitigating coordinate wrapping artifacts. The proposed architecture employs a tripartite fusion strategy: a SwinV2-Tiny transformer backbone for photometric feature extraction, a convolutional heatmap branch for spatial grounding, and a coordinate-based MLP for geometric anchoring. Experimental evaluations on a synthetic Hipparcos-derived dataset demonstrate that Star-Fusion achieves a Top-1 accuracy of 93.4% and a Top-3 accuracy of 97.8%. Furthermore, the model exhibits high computational efficiency, maintaining an inference latency of 18.4 ms on resource-constrained COTS hardware, making it a viable candidate for real-time onboard deployment in next-generation satellite constellations.

ai.tldr: 提出Star-Fusion多模态架构，将天体定向问题转化为离散拓扑分类任务，在合成数据集上达到93.4% Top-1精度，推理延迟仅18.4毫秒。
ai.motivation: 传统Lost-in-Space算法计算开销大且对噪声敏感，而标准回归模型难以处理天球非欧拓扑和周期性边界条件。
ai.method: 采用球面K-Means聚类将天球划分为K个拓扑一致区域，通过SwinV2-Tiny骨干提取光度特征、卷积热图分支进行空间定位、坐标MLP进行几何锚定的三分支融合架构。
ai.result: 在合成Hipparcos数据集上Top-1准确率93.4%，Top-3准确率97.8%，在资源受限硬件上推理延迟18.4毫秒。
ai.help_to_user: 为航天器自主导航提供一种高效、鲁棒的姿态确定方法，特别适用于资源受限的卫星星座实时部署。

idea_spark.idea: 将连续回归问题转化为离散分类任务，利用球面聚类消除坐标缠绕伪影，结合Transformer与卷积网络互补优势。
idea_spark.risk: 合成数据集与真实星图存在分布差异，实际部署时需考虑传感器噪声、星表不完整等挑战。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受球面K-Means聚类和Swin Transformer启发，将非欧几何先验融入深度学习架构设计。

zh.tldr: 提出Star-Fusion多模态架构，将天体定向问题转化为离散拓扑分类任务，在合成数据集上达到93.4% Top-1精度，推理延迟仅18.4毫秒。
zh.motivation: 传统Lost-in-Space算法计算开销大且对噪声敏感，而标准回归模型难以处理天球非欧拓扑和周期性边界条件。

idea_spark.idea: 将连续回归问题转化为离散分类任务，利用球面聚类消除坐标缠绕伪影，结合Transformer与卷积网络互补优势。

idea_spark.inspiration: 受球面K-Means聚类和Swin Transformer启发，将非欧几何先验融入深度学习架构设计。

en.tldr: Proposes Star-Fusion, a multi-modal architecture that reformulates celestial orientation as a discrete topological classification task, achieving 93.4% Top-1 accuracy and 18.4 ms inference latency on a synthetic dataset.
en.motivation: Traditional Lost-in-Space algorithms suffer from high computational cost and noise sensitivity, while standard regression models struggle with the non-Euclidean topology and periodic boundary conditions of the celestial sphere.
en.method: Employs spherical K-Means clustering to partition the celestial sphere into K topologically consistent regions, and a tripartite fusion strategy with a SwinV2-Tiny transformer backbone for photometric features, a convolutional heatmap branch for spatial grounding, and a coordinate-based MLP for geometric anchoring.
en.result: Achieves 93.4% Top-1 and 97.8% Top-3 accuracy on a synthetic Hipparcos-derived dataset, with 18.4 ms inference latency on resource-constrained COTS hardware.
en.help_to_user: Provides an efficient and robust attitude determination method for autonomous spacecraft navigation, suitable for real-time onboard deployment in next-generation satellite constellations.

idea_spark.idea: Convert continuous regression into a discrete classification task, use spherical clustering to eliminate coordinate wrapping artifacts, and combine Transformer and convolutional networks for complementary strengths.
idea_spark.risk: Distribution shift between synthetic and real star fields; real deployment must address sensor noise, incomplete star catalogs, and other challenges.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by spherical K-Means clustering and Swin Transformer, incorporating non-Euclidean geometric priors into deep learning architecture design.

papers.title: On the Capacity of Hierarchical Secure Aggregation with Groupwise Keys
papers.summary: We study the hierarchical secure aggregation problem with groupwise keys. The problem consists of an aggregation server, $U$ relays, and $UV$ users, where each relay serves $V$ disjoint users, and each subset of $G$ users shares an independent groupwise key. Two security requirements are imposed: relay security and server security. Specifically, each relay must not learn any information about the users' inputs, and the server must not learn any additional information beyond the recovered sum of all inputs.   We first show that the problem is infeasible when $G = 1$. For the feasible regime $1 < G \le UV$, we fully characterize the optimal rate region. In particular, we prove that both each user and each relay must transmit at least one symbol per input symbol. Furthermore, we characterize the minimum required groupwise key rate as $\max\left\{\frac{V}{\binom{UV}{G} - \binom{(U-1)V}{G}},\; \frac{U - 1}{\binom{UV}{G} - U \binom{V}{G}}\right\},$ where the two terms correspond to the constraints imposed by relay security and server security, respectively.   For achievability, we propose an explicit linear coding scheme based on structured precoding matrices, and show that it satisfies both correctness and security requirements. The construction avoids permutation-based symmetrization by leveraging sufficiently generic matrix designs over large fields. Finally, we establish a matching converse, thereby characterizing the optimal rate region.

ai.tldr: 研究了带分组密钥的分层安全聚合问题，完全刻画了最优速率区域，并给出了显式线性编码方案。
ai.motivation: 在分层聚合场景中，需要同时保护用户输入不被中继泄露，且服务器只能恢复总和，现有方案缺乏对分组密钥下最优速率的完整刻画。
ai.method: 通过信息论分析推导出中继安全和服务器安全分别对应的密钥率下界，并构造基于结构化预编码矩阵的线性编码方案实现可达性。
ai.result: 完全刻画了可行参数范围（G>1）下的最优速率区域，给出了最小分组密钥率的闭式表达式。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解分层安全聚合中分组密钥的理论极限，指导实际系统设计中的密钥分配和传输开销优化。

idea_spark.idea: 利用有限域上的通用矩阵设计替代排列对称化，简化编码结构的同时保持安全性。
idea_spark.risk: 大域上的矩阵设计可能带来计算复杂度增加，且分组密钥共享假设在实际部署中可能面临密钥分发挑战。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将安全约束分解为不同层级（中继、服务器）独立分析，再联合优化，这种分解思路可用于其他多级安全协议设计。

en.tldr: Studies hierarchical secure aggregation with groupwise keys, fully characterizes the optimal rate region, and provides an explicit linear coding scheme.
en.motivation: In hierarchical aggregation, both relay security and server security must be guaranteed; existing work lacks a complete characterization of optimal rates under groupwise key sharing.
en.method: Derives lower bounds on key rates from relay and server security constraints via information-theoretic analysis, and achieves them with a linear coding scheme using structured precoding matrices over large fields.
en.result: Fully characterizes the optimal rate region for feasible regime (G>1) and gives a closed-form expression for the minimum groupwise key rate.
en.help_to_user: Helps understand theoretical limits of hierarchical secure aggregation with groupwise keys, guiding key allocation and transmission overhead optimization in practical systems.

idea_spark.idea: Replace permutation-based symmetrization with generic matrix designs over large fields to simplify encoding while preserving security.
idea_spark.risk: Matrix design over large fields may increase computational complexity, and groupwise key sharing assumptions may face key distribution challenges in practice.
idea_spark.inspiration: Decomposing security constraints into separate layers (relay, server) and then jointly optimizing can inspire multi-level security protocol design.

papers.title: CogRAG+: Cognitive-Level Guided Diagnosis and Remediation of Memory and Reasoning Deficiencies in Professional Exam QA
papers.summary: Professional domain knowledge underpins human civilization, serving as both the basis for industry entry and the core of complex decision-making and problem-solving. However, existing large language models often suffer from opaque inference processes in which retrieval and reasoning are tightly entangled, causing knowledge gaps and reasoning inconsistencies in professional tasks. To address this, we propose CogRAG+, a training-free framework that decouples and aligns the retrieval-augmented generation pipeline with human cognitive hierarchies. First, we introduce Reinforced Retrieval, a judge-driven dual-path strategy with fact-centric and option-centric paths that strengthens retrieval and mitigates cascading failures caused by missing foundational knowledge. We then develop cognition-stratified Constrained Reasoning, which replaces unconstrained chain-of-thought generation with structured templates to reduce logical inconsistency and generative redundancy. Experiments on two representative models, Qwen3-8B and Llama3.1-8B, show that CogRAG+ consistently outperforms general-purpose models and standard RAG methods on the Registered Dietitian qualification exam. In single-question mode, it raises overall accuracy to 85.8\% for Qwen3-8B and 60.3\% for Llama3.1-8B, with clear gains over vanilla baselines. Constrained Reasoning also reduces the unanswered rate from 7.6\% to 1.4\%. CogRAG+ offers a robust, model-agnostic path toward training-free expert-level performance in specialized domains.

ai.tldr: 提出CogRAG+框架，通过解耦检索与推理、引入强化检索和认知分层约束推理，提升专业考试问答中大型语言模型的准确性和推理一致性。
ai.motivation: 现有大语言模型在专业任务中检索与推理紧密耦合，导致知识缺失和推理不一致，缺乏透明推理过程。
ai.method: 采用无训练框架，包含强化检索（事实中心与选项中心双路径）和认知分层约束推理（结构化模板替代自由链式思维）。
ai.result: 在注册营养师资格考试中，Qwen3-8B准确率达85.8%，Llama3.1-8B达60.3%，未回答率从7.6%降至1.4%。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种无需额外训练即可提升专业领域问答性能的通用方法，适用于需要可靠知识检索和逻辑推理的场景。

idea_spark.idea: 将认知分层思想与强化学习结合，设计自适应检索策略，根据问题难度动态调整检索深度和推理模板。
idea_spark.risk: 结构化模板可能限制创造性推理，在开放性问题中表现不佳；强化检索依赖高质量判断器，可能引入偏见。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受人类认知层次启发，将复杂任务分解为检索和推理阶段，并分别优化，避免端到端黑箱模型。

en.tldr: Proposes CogRAG+, a training-free framework that decouples retrieval and reasoning, using reinforced retrieval and cognition-stratified constrained reasoning to improve accuracy and consistency in professional exam QA.
en.motivation: Existing LLMs suffer from opaque inference and entangled retrieval-reasoning, causing knowledge gaps and inconsistencies in professional tasks.
en.method: Training-free framework with reinforced retrieval (fact-centric and option-centric dual paths) and cognition-stratified constrained reasoning (structured templates replacing free chain-of-thought).
en.result: On Registered Dietitian exam, Qwen3-8B achieves 85.8% accuracy, Llama3.1-8B 60.3%, and unanswered rate drops from 7.6% to 1.4%.
en.help_to_user: Provides a model-agnostic, training-free approach to boost performance in specialized domain QA, useful for applications requiring reliable knowledge retrieval and logical reasoning.

idea_spark.idea: Combine cognitive stratification with reinforcement learning to design adaptive retrieval strategies that adjust depth and reasoning templates based on question difficulty.
idea_spark.risk: Structured templates may limit creative reasoning in open-ended questions; reinforced retrieval depends on high-quality judges, potentially introducing bias.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by human cognitive hierarchy, decomposing complex tasks into retrieval and reasoning stages for separate optimization, avoiding end-to-end black-box models.

papers.title: When Hidden States Drift: Can KV Caches Rescue Long-Range Speculative Decoding?
papers.summary: Speculative decoding accelerates LLM inference, but SOTA hidden-state-based drafters suffer from long-range decay: draft accuracy degrades as the speculative step increases. Existing work attributes this decay to train-inference mismatch and proposes test-time training (TTT) as a remedy, yet we observe that long-range decay persists even in TTT-trained drafters. We revisit long-range decay from the perspective of context information preservation. In hidden-state reuse, we argue the target hidden state acts as a biased context compression: it aggregates historical token information according to the attention query at the current position, yielding a compact representation optimized for immediate next-token prediction. This compression can suppress information less relevant to the current query but important for later speculative steps. In contrast, the target model's KV cache serves as an explicit context, retaining the complete set of token-wise KV representations. We therefore posit the KV-Reuse Hypothesis: allowing the draft model to reuse the target KV cache can provide richer signals for long-horizon drafting. To test this hypothesis, we introduce KVShot, a diagnostic framework that compares three reuse paradigms: hidden-only, KV-only, and hybrid. Extensive evaluations on Qwen3-8B show that KV-Reuse improves long-range acceptance, although end-to-end speedups remain marginal under current training pipelines. Our analysis identifies two key structural bottlenecks: shallow drafters struggle to estimate target queries accurately, and draft-side KV projections receive sparse gradient signals. These findings suggest that realizing the full potential of KV-aware decoding requires moving beyond TTT toward block-wise training paradigms. By exposing these bottlenecks, KVShot provides a foundational diagnostic testbed and a clear roadmap for designing next-generation inference architectures.

ai.tldr: 本文发现基于隐藏状态推测解码中的长程衰减问题，提出KV缓存重用假设，并通过KVShot诊断框架验证，指出当前训练范式的瓶颈。
ai.motivation: 现有推测解码中隐藏状态复用存在长程衰减，即使测试时训练也无法解决，需探索KV缓存作为更丰富的上下文信号。
ai.method: 提出KVShot诊断框架，比较隐藏状态复用、KV缓存复用和混合复用三种范式，在Qwen3-8B上评估。
ai.result: KV缓存复用提升长程接受率，但端到端加速有限，主要瓶颈在于浅层推测模型难以准确估计目标查询以及KV投影梯度稀疏。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解推测解码中长程衰减的根本原因，并为设计下一代推理架构提供诊断工具和方向。

idea_spark.idea: 设计块级训练范式，让推测模型在训练时直接利用目标KV缓存，而非仅依赖隐藏状态。
idea_spark.risk: 块级训练可能增加计算开销，且需要重新设计训练目标以避免过拟合。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从上下文信息保留角度重新审视推测解码，启发将显式记忆机制（如KV缓存）融入轻量级推测模型。

en.tldr: This paper identifies long-range decay in hidden-state-based speculative decoding, proposes the KV-Reuse hypothesis, and validates it via the KVShot diagnostic framework, revealing bottlenecks in current training paradigms.
en.motivation: Existing hidden-state reuse suffers from long-range decay even with test-time training, motivating exploration of KV cache as a richer context signal.
en.method: Introduces KVShot, a diagnostic framework comparing hidden-only, KV-only, and hybrid reuse paradigms, evaluated on Qwen3-8B.
en.result: KV-Reuse improves long-range acceptance but yields marginal end-to-end speedups; key bottlenecks are inaccurate target query estimation by shallow drafters and sparse gradient signals for draft-side KV projections.
en.help_to_user: Helps understand the root cause of long-range decay in speculative decoding and provides a diagnostic tool and roadmap for next-generation inference architectures.

idea_spark.idea: Design block-wise training paradigms where the draft model directly leverages the target KV cache during training, rather than relying solely on hidden states.
idea_spark.risk: Block-wise training may increase computational cost and require careful training objective design to avoid overfitting.
idea_spark.inspiration: Revisiting speculative decoding from the perspective of context information preservation inspires incorporating explicit memory mechanisms (e.g., KV cache) into lightweight draft models.

papers.title: Last-Layer-Centric Feature Recombination: Unleashing 3D Geometric Knowledge in DINOv3 for Monocular Depth Estimation
papers.summary: Monocular depth estimation (MDE) is a fundamental yet inherently ill-posed task. Recent vision foundation models (VFMs), particularly DINO-based transformers, have significantly improved accuracy and generalization for dense prediction. Prior works generally follow a unified paradigm: sampling a fixed set of intermediate transformer layers at uniform intervals to build multi-scale features. This common practice implicitly assumes that geometric information is uniformly distributed across layers, which may underutilize the structural 3D cues encoded in VFMs. In this study, we present a systematic layer-wise analysis of DINOv3, revealing that 3D information is distributed non-uniformly: deeper layers exhibit stronger depth predictability and better capture inter-sample geometric variation. Motivated by this, we introduce a Last-Layer-Centric Feature Recombination (LFR) module to enhance geometric expressiveness. LFR treats the final layer as a geometric anchor and adaptively selects complementary intermediate layers according to a minimal-similarity criterion. Selected features are fused with the last-layer representation via compact linear adapters.Extensive experiments show that LFR module consistently improves MDE accuracy and achieves state-of-the-art performance. Our analysis sheds light on how geometric knowledge is organized within VFMs and offers an efficient strategy for unlocking their potential in dense 3D tasks.

ai.tldr: 提出以最后一层为中心的LFR模块，通过自适应选择互补中间层特征，提升单目深度估计精度。
ai.motivation: 现有方法均匀采样中间层特征，未充分利用VFM中非均匀分布的3D几何信息。
ai.method: 系统分析DINOv3各层深度预测能力，以最后一层为几何锚点，按最小相似度准则选择互补中间层，通过线性适配器融合。
ai.result: LFR模块持续提升MDE精度，达到当前最优性能。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种高效利用VFM几何知识进行密集3D任务的策略。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将最后一层锚点替换为多锚点或动态锚点，以适应不同场景的几何复杂度。
idea_spark.risk: 线性适配器可能限制跨层特征的表达能力，需验证更复杂融合机制的效果。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受层间几何信息非均匀分布启发，可进一步研究VFM中知识组织的层级结构。

zh.tldr: 提出以最后一层为中心的LFR模块，通过自适应选择互补中间层特征，提升单目深度估计精度。
zh.motivation: 现有方法均匀采样中间层特征，未充分利用VFM中非均匀分布的3D几何信息。
zh.method: 系统分析DINOv3各层深度预测能力，以最后一层为几何锚点，按最小相似度准则选择互补中间层，通过线性适配器融合。
